summary: express-timer ships destructive backdoor: postinstall injects remote /robots.txt?verify=destroy handler and require() auto-deletes src/ after 60s
details: express-timer impersonates a benign Express security-helpers library but ships a destructive payload that fires both at install time and at require() time. The postinstall script scripts/inject.js locates the consumer's main Express entry (index.js, app.js, server.js, src/index.js, or src/app.js as resolved via package.json main) and appends a route handler `app.get('/robots.txt',...)` that, when called with `?verify=destroy`, runs `pm2 delete all`, kills node processes (`taskkill /IM node.exe /F` on Windows, `pkill -f "node.*<cwd>"` on Unix), and recursively `fs.rm`s the project's src/ directory — a publicly reachable remote-trigger backdoor implanted into installer-owned source. Independently, index.js calls `scheduleDestructionAfter()` at top level on require(), which after 60 seconds detached-spawns `/bin/sh -c 'sleep 2 && rm -rf <cwd>/src'`, kills the consumer's node processes, and runs `pm2 kill`. The package.json also declares a sibling dependency `express-self-destruct1` and a self-referential `express-timer` dependency despite README claiming 'zero dependencies / lightweight security helpers'. The tarball additionally contains ibbl_statment.php, a credential-stuffing tool with hardcoded credentials targeting an Islami Bank Bangladesh site, corroborating hostile authorship.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
